title: Agricultural Foundation / 农本思想
summary: Mencius's governance principle that agricultural development is fundamental to state prosperity

title: Learning and Its Practice / 学而时习之
summary: The opening chapter of the Analects emphasizing the importance of continuous learning, review, and friendship in personal cultivation.

title: Ba Yi (Eight Rows) / 八佾
summary: The ceremonial dance of eight rows of eight performers, a symbol of ritual order and social hierarchy in ancient Chinese rites.

title: Benevolence / 仁
summary: Discusses the foundational role of benevolence in Confucian ethics and its implications for interpersonal relationships and governance.

title: Benevolent Government / 仁政
summary: Benevolent government is characterized by a ruler's compassion and morality, leading to harmony in society.

title: Brahma's Fourfold Teaching on Liberation / 梵天王四法
summary: Mahābrahmā's teachings on the four conditions for rebirth in the Brahma realm: non-self practice, one-pointedness of mind, compassionate liberation, and abandoning defilements.

title: Chang Ahan Jing (Long Discourses of the Buddha) / 長阿含經
summary: The Dīrgha-āgama, the longest collection of the Buddha's long discourses in early Buddhist scripture, compiled in four sections and five recitations.

title: Chaou K'e / 赵岐
summary: Han dynasty scholar who created the first major commentary on Mencius

title: Confucian View of Music (Yue) / 儒家的音乐观（乐）
summary: Confucian philosophy on the moral and social functions of music in maintaining ritual order and personal cultivation.

title: Self-Examination in Confucianism / 三省吾身
summary: The practice of daily self-reflection as a fundamental method of moral cultivation in Confucian thought.

title: Confucius's Etiquette in Public Life / 孔子乡党礼仪
summary: Chapter 10 of the Analects describing Confucius's formal behavior and ritual propriety in various social contexts including village, court, ceremonies, and interactions with different ranks of people.

title: Eightfold Noble Path (Human Immortal Sutra) / 八正道
summary: The Eightfold Noble Path as expounded in the Sutra of the Human Immortal, taught by Mahabrahma Deva as the method for achieving pure contemplation and liberation.

title: Fixed Livelihood / 恒产论
summary: Mencius's economic theory that stable property ownership is essential for moral stability

title: Four Bases of Supernatural Power / 四神足
summary: The Four Bases of Supernatural Power (rddhipāda) taught by Mahabrahma Deva, describing the spiritual faculties of desire, endeavor, mind, and investigation.

title: Buddhist Origin of the Four Castes / 四姓起源
summary: Buddhist cosmological account of how the four varnas (brahman, kshatriya, vaishya, shudra) arose from primordial beings and the Buddha's teaching on karmic equality.

title: Governance and Leadership / 治理与领导
summary: Analyzes the principles of governance and leadership in the context of Confucian ethics, focusing on the responsibilities and virtues of leaders.

title: Governance through Virtue / 为政以德
summary: Confucius's political philosophy emphasizing rule through moral virtue and ethical leadership rather than coercion or punishment.

title: The Origin Story of the Great Firm Brāhmaṇa / 大堅固婆羅門緣起
summary: A Buddhist sūtra recounting how King Linu's minister Mahākakuda meets the Buddha, attains stream-entry, renounces worldly life, and becomes an arhat, inspiring seven kings, seven thousand brāhmaṇas, and countless laypeople to follow the Dharma.

title: The Great Firm Brahman / 大堅固婆羅門
summary: A jātaka-like narrative about a minister named Humeng who became an arhat through renunciation and taught the Dharma, later identified as a past life of the Buddha.

title: Han Dynasty Reception of Mencius / 汉代孟子接受史
summary: Historical process of Mencius's recognition and preservation during the Han dynasty

title: Human Immortal / 人仙
summary: A celestial being who is the son of Vaisravana (毘沙門天王) and serves as a messenger revealing the karmic fate of King Bimbisara.

title: human-nature-is-good
Human Nature / 人性
summary: Exploration of the concept of human nature as viewed by Mencius within Confucian philosophy.

title: Universal Love (兼愛) / 兼愛
summary: The Mohist principle advocating equal care for all people without distinction, considered the foundation of Mozi's ethical philosophy.

title: The Role of the Junzi / 君子的角色
summary: Examines the characteristics and responsibilities of the Junzi in Confucian philosophy.

title: Knowledge and Learning / 知识与学习
summary: Explores the significance of knowledge and learning in Confucian thought and their implications for personal growth and governance.

title: mendicant-life-three-conditions
Mencius / 孟子
summary: Mencius (c. 372–289 BCE) was the most prominent Confucian philosopher after Confucius, known for his doctrine of the innate goodness of human nature.

title: Ministerial Advice / 大臣的劝谏
summary: The role of ministerial advice is crucial in ensuring benevolent governance and societal harmony.

title: Moral Responsibility / 道德责任
summary: Moral responsibility emphasizes the ethical obligations individuals hold towards society and each other.

title: Seven Buddhas of the Current Kalpa / 七佛
summary: The seven Buddhas who have appeared in the current kalpa, from Vipassin to Shakyamuni.

title: Tang Wan Kung / 唐宓公
summary: Overview of the historical and philosophical significance of Tang Wan Kung in Confucianism.

title: Eight Rare Qualities of a Tathagata / 如來八希有法
summary: Eight exceptional qualities that appear when a Buddha enters the world, as taught by Indra and repeated by Brahma, forming a unique doctrinal teaching of this sutra.

title: Trayastriṃśa Heaven / 三十三天（忉利天）
summary: The Heaven of Thirty-Three Gods where the initial discourse of the Great Firm Brāhmaṇa Sūtra takes place, presided over by Śakra (帝釋天主) and the site of the first teaching of the eight rare qualities.

title: Three Years Mourning / 三年之丧
summary: The traditional mourning period of three years emphasizes filial piety and respect for deceased parents.

title: Twelve Links of Dependent Origination / 十二因緣
summary: The Buddhist doctrine explaining the cyclical chain of causation from ignorance to suffering.

title: Virtue and Ethics / 德与伦理
summary: Discusses the importance of virtue in Confucian ethics and its practical applications in governance and personal conduct.

title: Will and Passion-Nature / 志與情的關係
summary: Mencius's doctrine on the hierarchical yet interactive relationship between will (zhi) and passion-nature (qing)
